name: Baked Pea Beans
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak washed and sorted beans overnight.
2. Drain and add fresh water to come 3 inches above the beans. Add the onion, cloves, bay leaf, and salt, and bring to a boil. Boil rapidly for 5 minutes. Skim the top, reduce heat, and simmer until the beans are tender but not mushy.
3. Meanwhile, put the salt pork in water and bring to a boil. Lower the heat and simmer for 25 minutes. Remove the pork from the water and cut it first into slices, then into small pieces. Cut the ham into strips.
4. Peel and chop the onions and garlic cloves. Blend the mustard with a little bean liquid. Taste the beans for seasoning.
5. Place a layer of the beans in the bottom of a large baking dish. Add a layer of salt pork, ham, onion, and garlic. Repeat the layers ending with beans on top. Pour the mustard water over the mixture, and add enough bean liquid to come to the top of the baking dish.
6. Bake in a preheated moderate oven (350°F) for 90 minutes.
7. Keep the beans moist while baking by adding more bean liquid or water if needed.
8. After 90 minutes, remove and pour any remaining liquid over the beans. Return to the oven and bake another 45 minutes, or until nicely browned and bubbly. Reheat at a picnic on a camp stove or serve cold.
